headline: Transcript: Assisted dying — the vote
summary: Lucy Fisher talks to George Parker, Stephen Bush and Laura Hughes
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Political Fix podcast episode: ‘Assisted dying — the vote’

Lucy Fisher How big a shift will it be if the UK passes assisted dying?

Laura Hughes It will be a historic moment in parliament and also in the country.

Stephen Bush There’ll, I think, be a momentous shift in the type of country we are, if it happens.

George Parker I think if you go all the way back to the ’60s, don’t you, and the debates on capital punishment and abortion to find something so seismic and so momentous for this country.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Lucy Fisher Welcome to Political Fix from the Financial Times with me, Lucy Fisher.

MPs will vote on assisted dying for the first time in nearly a decade on Friday. It could pave the way to one of the most significant social changes of our time. And parliamentarians are deeply divided over this emotive issue. Meanwhile, new migration stats show that net arrivals to the UK hit a record of 900,000 people last year, far higher than previously
